title: Kalshi Market Candle (OHLCV)
description: Candlestick data for Kalshi prediction markets. Sparse storage: only candles with complete OHLC (actual trades) are stored. Canonical storage uses CENTS (float 0–100) consistent with book_snapshot.json and market_unified.json. Parse Kalshi FixedPointDollars strings via: float(api_string) * 100.

period_interval.description: Candle interval in MINUTES. Note: Go API uses seconds (60, 300, 900, 1800, 3600, 86400). JSON Schema canonical unit is minutes.

end_period_ts.description: End of candle period (RFC3339 UTC). Aligned to interval boundary.

open.description: Opening price in CENTS (0.0–100.0). Kalshi API returns FixedPointDollars strings (e.g., '0.5600' = 56 cents). Parse: float(api_string) * 100. Subpenny precision is preserved as decimal (e.g., 56.7 cents for deci-cent tick markets).

high.description: Highest price in cents. Must be >= open, low, close.

low.description: Lowest price in cents. Must be <= open, high, close.

volume.description: Number of contracts traded during period.

open_interest.description: Open interest (outstanding contracts) at end of period.

filled.description: True if this candle was forward-filled (no actual trade, OHLC copied from previous close). Default false.

$comment: BUSINESS RULES: (1) high >= max(open, low, close). (2) low <= min(open, high, close). (3) Only complete OHLC candles stored (sparse). (4) KALSHI API MIGRATION (March 12, 2026): Legacy integer cents fields REMOVED. API now returns FixedPointDollars strings. MostlyRight stores as float cents [0, 100]. Conversion: cents = float(dollars_string) * 100. (5) Unit consistency: candle.json, book_snapshot.json, and market_unified.json all use cents. (6) On conflict: full overwrite (no COALESCE). (7) The price_level_structure field on each market indicates tick size.
